title: North Rauceby T.2834 Memorial
crew_summary: 2 killed; pilot Plt Off. Henry Eric Rath

external_narratives.summary: Detailed account of the loss of Wellington T2834 from No. 3 Coastal Operational Training Unit at RAF Cranwell on 20 April 1942. P/O Henry Eric Rath (J/6686 RCAF, age 21) and Sgt Karl Morris Miegel (AUS/403480 RAAF, age 27) were conducting night circuit and landing practice when the aircraft flew into high ground near the vicarage at North Rauceby at 0120 hours.
key_details: Rath buried in Cranwell Churchyard (St Andrew), Plot 3 Row E Grave 1
external_narratives.full_narrative:
• Kracker Archive • Allied Losses • Archiwum Polish • Paradie Canadian • RCAF • RAAF • RNZAF • USA • Searchable Lists Add to or correct this story with a few clicks. Archive Report: Allied Forces Compiled from official National Archive and Service sources, contemporary press reports, personal logbooks, diaries and correspondence, reference books, other sources, and interviews. Check our Research databases: Database List . We seek additional information and photographs. Please contact us via the Helpdesk. 20.04.1942 No. 3 Operational Training Unit Wellington T2834, P/O. Rath Operation: Training

Date: 20th April 1942 (Monday)

Unit: No. 3 Operational Training Unit (Coastal Command)

Location: North Rauceby

Pilot: P/O. Henry Eric Rath J/6686 RCAF Age 21. Killed

Pilot: Sgt. Karl Morris Miegel AUS/403480 RAAF Age 27. Killed

During a training exercise the two pilots were instructed to do circuits carrying out landings at night and circuits. They were apparently instructed to abort the exercise if the local visibility became too low.

At 01:20 hrs. the Wellington flew into high ground near the vicarage at North Rauceby - the aircraft burnt out, killing both pilots.

P/O. Henry Eric Rath. Cranwell Churchyard (St. Andrew) Plot 3. Row E. Grave 1. Son of Samuel S. Rath of Stafford Township and husband of Lillian Edna Rath (née Sutherland), of 540 Gladstone Avenue, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada. Grave inscription reads: " The Eternal God Is Thy Refuge And Underneath Are The Everlasting Arms".

Sgt. Karl Morris Miegel. Cranwell Churchyard (St. Andrew) Plot 3. Row D. Grave 1. Son of Karl Theodor and Sarah Jane Miegel, of Wahroonga, New South Wales. Australia. Grave inscription reads: " We Lost The Roses And Sunshine When We Lost You".

For further details our thanks to the following sources shown below.

external_narratives.summary: RAF Commands archive entry providing official records for Wellington T2834 and the circumstances of the training accident at North Rauceby.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Gillian Enstone via Ian Grady: the map is not of North Rauceby (Wave 1 CRM response)
Postcodes.io + Photon OSM: North Rauceby North Kesteven NG34 at 53.006, -0.478
location_confidence.verified_by: Gillian Enstone / Ian Grady (local contacts), Holly (PAI geocoding)
location_confidence.verification_notes: Previous coords (53.151, -0.197) were ~16km north and ~24km east of North Rauceby. Village near Sleaford NG34 confirmed. Precise memorial location within village TBC.
